preamble: Monophthongization of 'ai' to 'e' is a common shift where a low-to-high diphthong simplifies to a mid front vowel, often through assimilation of the two elements.

examples.note: Original diphthongs /ai/ and /au/ became long mid vowels /e:/ and /o:/ in Classical Sanskrit.
Latin diphthong AI frequently monophthongized to /e/ or /ɛ/ in many Romance dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
